key_themes: Record 29% revenue growth (£229m) with 10% organic and full-year Peters Surgical contribution
Margin dilution (adj. PBT -1.8pp to 14.8%) from acquisition mix, restructuring, and tariffs offset by strong cash flow (+67%) and synergies
Bullish analyst consensus (Buy ratings, 270-300p targets implying 20-35% upside), positive outlook with R&D pipeline and 2026 guidance in line with expectations
summary: AMS delivered strong FY2025 results with record revenue up 29% to £228.9m (10% organic constant currency growth in the legacy business plus Peters Surgical), adjusted EPS +12%, reported profit before tax +81%, robust cash generation that reduced net debt, and a 10% dividend increase. Integration of the acquisition is progressing well with commercial synergies already visible and £10m+ annual operational benefits targeted from 2027; the R&D pipeline has multiple approvals lined up for 2026-27 supporting further Surgical strength and Woundcare recovery.[[1]](https://admedsol.com/regulatory-news-announcements/7921351/)[[2]](https://admedsol.com/investor-relations/financial-highlights/)[[3]](https://www.investorschronicle.co.uk/content/7427af7b-5820-4f2a-81d1-de07c4e302d9) Analysts maintain Buy ratings with targets well above the recent ~220p share price, but the biggest risk is sustained or slower-than-expected margin recovery given dilution from lower-margin acquired assets, restructuring costs, inventory normalization, US tariffs, and currency effects.[[4]](https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/advanced-medical-solutions-group-lonams-stock-price-passes-below-200-day-moving-average-heres-what-happened-2026-04-03/)

recent_posts.highlight: Record sales, strong organic growth in Surgical (+35%) and Woundcare recovery (+9%), confident in delivering 2026 consensus (£245m revenue, £55m adj EBITDA), synergies on track
Consensus Buy rating with ~274p target; Berenberg Buy at 300p (significant upside from current levels); shares reacted positively to results and dividend hike
Notes 'better quality growth' post-acquisition integration and rising dividend; market reaction positive
Shares potentially 50% below intrinsic value but 'returns on capital signal tricky times ahead' given low ROE (~4%) and margin pressure
